003 * contributor license agreements.  See the NOTICE file distributed with
004 * this work for additional information regarding copyright ownership.
005 * The ASF licenses this file to You under the Apache License, Version 2.0
006 * (the "License"); you may not use this file except in compliance with
007 * the License.  You may obtain a copy of the License at
008 *
009 *      http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
010 *
011 * Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
012 * distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
013 * WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
014 * See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
015 * limitations under the License.
016 */
017package org.apache.juneau.marshall;
018
019import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.ThrowableUtils.*;
020
021import java.time.*;
022import java.time.format.*;
023import java.util.*;
024
025import javax.xml.datatype.*;
026
027import org.apache.juneau.marshall.swaps.*;
028
029/**
030 * Supported wire formats for {@link Calendar} values.
031 *
032 * <p>
033 * Used by {@link MarshallingContext.Builder#calendarFormat(CalendarFormat)},
034 * {@link Marshalled#calendarFormat()},
035 * {@link MarshalledProp#calendarFormat()}, and
036 * {@link MarshalledConfig#calendarFormat()} to control how
037 * {@link Calendar} values (including {@link GregorianCalendar}) are written to and read from the wire.
038 *
039 * <p>
040 * The default is {@link #ISO_OFFSET_DATE_TIME} which preserves the historical wire output produced
041 * before this enum existed.
042 *
043 * <p>
044 * Numeric formats ({@link #MILLIS}) emit native numeric wire types on binary serializers
045 * (BSON / CBOR / MsgPack) and bare numbers on text formats. Text formats emit a quoted ISO 8601 string for
046 * every other constant.
047 *
048 * <p>
049 * {@link XMLGregorianCalendar} fields always emit {@code toXMLFormat()} regardless of the configured
050 * {@code CalendarFormat}. The {@link #XML_FORMAT} constant exists so that regular {@code Calendar} /
051 * {@code GregorianCalendar} fields can opt into the same wire form for symmetry with their
052 * {@code XMLGregorianCalendar} siblings.
053 */
054public enum CalendarFormat {
055
056   /** Sentinel meaning "no value configured" — falls through to the next-higher precedence level. */
057   NOT_SET,
058
059   /** {@link DateTimeFormatter#BASIC_ISO_DATE}: <js>"20111203"</js>. */
060   BASIC_ISO_DATE,
061
062   /** {@link DateTimeFormatter#ISO_DATE}: <js>"2011-12-03+01:00"</js> or <js>"2011-12-03"</js>. */
063   ISO_DATE,
064
065   /** {@link DateTimeFormatter#ISO_DATE_TIME}: <js>"2011-12-03T10:15:30+01:00[Europe/Paris]"</js>. */
066   ISO_DATE_TIME,
067
068   /** {@link DateTimeFormatter#ISO_INSTANT}: <js>"2011-12-03T10:15:30Z"</js>. */
069   ISO_INSTANT,
070
071   /** {@link DateTimeFormatter#ISO_LOCAL_DATE}: <js>"2011-12-03"</js>. */
072   ISO_LOCAL_DATE,
073
074   /** {@link DateTimeFormatter#ISO_LOCAL_DATE_TIME}: <js>"2011-12-03T10:15:30"</js>. */
075   ISO_LOCAL_DATE_TIME,
076
077   /** {@link DateTimeFormatter#ISO_LOCAL_TIME}: <js>"10:15:30"</js>. */
078   ISO_LOCAL_TIME,
079
080   /** {@link DateTimeFormatter#ISO_OFFSET_DATE}: <js>"2011-12-03+01:00"</js>. */
081   ISO_OFFSET_DATE,
082
083   /** {@link DateTimeFormatter#ISO_OFFSET_DATE_TIME} (the default): <js>"2011-12-03T10:15:30+01:00"</js>. */
084   ISO_OFFSET_DATE_TIME,
085
086   /** {@link DateTimeFormatter#ISO_OFFSET_TIME}: <js>"10:15:30+01:00"</js>. */
087   ISO_OFFSET_TIME,
088
089   /** {@link DateTimeFormatter#ISO_ORDINAL_DATE}: <js>"2012-337"</js>. */
090   ISO_ORDINAL_DATE,
091
092   /** {@link DateTimeFormatter#ISO_TIME}: <js>"10:15:30+01:00"</js> or <js>"10:15:30"</js>. */
093   ISO_TIME,
094
095   /** {@link DateTimeFormatter#ISO_WEEK_DATE}: <js>"2012-W48-6"</js>. */
096   ISO_WEEK_DATE,
097
098   /** {@link DateTimeFormatter#ISO_ZONED_DATE_TIME}: <js>"2011-12-03T10:15:30+01:00[Europe/Paris]"</js>. */
099   ISO_ZONED_DATE_TIME,
100
101   /** {@link DateTimeFormatter#RFC_1123_DATE_TIME}: <js>"Tue, 3 Jun 2008 11:05:30 GMT"</js>. */
102   RFC_1123_DATE_TIME,
103
104   /**
105    * Epoch milliseconds as a numeric value.
106    *
107    * <p>
108    * Emitted as a native int64 by binary serializers (BSON / CBOR / MsgPack) and as a bare integer
109    * literal by text serializers.
110    */
111   MILLIS,
112
113   /**
114    * {@link XMLGregorianCalendar#toXMLFormat() XML Schema dateTime} form.
115    *
116    * <p>
117    * Lets regular {@link Calendar} / {@link GregorianCalendar} fields opt into the same wire form
118    * that {@link XMLGregorianCalendar} fields always use, for symmetry across mixed-type beans.
119    */
120   XML_FORMAT;
121
122   private static final ZoneId Z = ZoneId.of("Z");
123
124   private static final DatatypeFactory DATATYPE_FACTORY;
125   static {
126      try {
127         DATATYPE_FACTORY = DatatypeFactory.newInstance();
128      } catch (DatatypeConfigurationException e) {
129         throw toRex(e);
130      }
131   }
132
133   /**
134    * Formats the specified value using this format.
135    *
136    * @param value The value to format. Can be <jk>null</jk>.
137    * @param zoneId The default zone id used for time-only / unzoned values.
138    * @return The formatted string, or <jk>null</jk> if {@code value} is <jk>null</jk>.
139    */
140   public String format(Calendar value, ZoneId zoneId) {
141      if (value == null)
142         return null;
143      if (this == MILLIS)
144         return Long.toString(value.getTimeInMillis());
145      if (this == XML_FORMAT) {
146         var gc = value instanceof GregorianCalendar gc2 ? gc2 : GregorianCalendar.from(value.toInstant().atZone(value.getTimeZone().toZoneId()));
147         return DATATYPE_FACTORY.newXMLGregorianCalendar(gc).toXMLFormat();
148      }
149      var zone = zoneId == null ? value.getTimeZone().toZoneId() : zoneId;
150      var zdt = value instanceof GregorianCalendar gc ? gc.toZonedDateTime() : value.toInstant().atZone(zone);
151      return formatter().format(zdt);
152   }
153
154   /**
155    * Parses the specified wire value using this format.
156    *
157    * @param value The wire value. Can be <jk>null</jk> or blank.
158    * @param zoneId The default zone id used for time-only / unzoned values.
159    * @return The parsed {@link Calendar}, or <jk>null</jk> if {@code value} is <jk>null</jk> or blank.
160    */
161   public Calendar parse(String value, ZoneId zoneId) {
162      if (value == null)
163         return null;
164      var s = value.trim();
165      if (s.isEmpty())
166         return null;
167      var zone = zoneId == null ? ZoneId.systemDefault() : zoneId;
168      if (this == MILLIS)
169         return GregorianCalendar.from(Instant.ofEpochMilli(Long.parseLong(s)).atZone(zone));
170      if (this == XML_FORMAT)
171         return DATATYPE_FACTORY.newXMLGregorianCalendar(s).toGregorianCalendar();
172      var ta = new DefaultingTemporalAccessor(formatter().parse(s), zone);
173      return GregorianCalendar.from(ZonedDateTime.from(ta));
174   }
175
176   /**
177    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if this format is numeric on the wire.
178    *
179    * @return <jk>true</jk> if this format emits a numeric wire value.
180    */
181   public boolean isNumeric() {
182      return this == MILLIS;
183   }
184
185   private DateTimeFormatter formatter() {
186      return switch (this) {
187         case NOT_SET, ISO_OFFSET_DATE_TIME -> DateTimeFormatter.ISO_OFFSET_DATE_TIME;
188         case BASIC_ISO_DATE -> DateTimeFormatter.BASIC_ISO_DATE;
189         case ISO_DATE -> DateTimeFormatter.ISO_DATE;
190         case ISO_DATE_TIME -> DateTimeFormatter.ISO_DATE_TIME;
191         case ISO_INSTANT -> DateTimeFormatter.ISO_INSTANT;
192         case ISO_LOCAL_DATE -> DateTimeFormatter.ISO_LOCAL_DATE;
193         case ISO_LOCAL_DATE_TIME -> DateTimeFormatter.ISO_LOCAL_DATE_TIME;
194         case ISO_LOCAL_TIME -> DateTimeFormatter.ISO_LOCAL_TIME;
195         case ISO_OFFSET_DATE -> DateTimeFormatter.ISO_OFFSET_DATE;
196         case ISO_OFFSET_TIME -> DateTimeFormatter.ISO_OFFSET_TIME;
197         case ISO_ORDINAL_DATE -> DateTimeFormatter.ISO_ORDINAL_DATE;
198         case ISO_TIME -> DateTimeFormatter.ISO_TIME;
199         case ISO_WEEK_DATE -> DateTimeFormatter.ISO_WEEK_DATE;
200         case ISO_ZONED_DATE_TIME -> DateTimeFormatter.ISO_ZONED_DATE_TIME;
201         case RFC_1123_DATE_TIME -> DateTimeFormatter.RFC_1123_DATE_TIME;
202         case XML_FORMAT, MILLIS -> throw new IllegalStateException("Format " + this + " does not use a DateTimeFormatter");
203      };
204   }
205
206   /**
207    * Returns the {@link ZoneId} used by the {@link #ISO_INSTANT} variant.
208    *
209    * @return The fixed {@code "Z"} (UTC) zone id used by the {@link #ISO_INSTANT} variant.
210    */
211   public static ZoneId zuluZone() {
212      return Z;
213   }
214}
